changes_made: Clarified function names to better reflect their purpose and ensure consistency with naming conventions.
review_notes: The functions are relevant to the control SC-12(1) as they address cryptographic key management in Kubernetes. However, minor adjustments in naming were necessary to enhance clarity and consistency with established patterns.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly indicate the specific action or feature being checked, such as 'rotation' and 'integration'.
title: Cryptographic Key Establishment And Management - Cryptographic key establishment and management | availability maintain availabil
step1_reasoning: The control SC-12(1) focuses on cryptographic key establishment and management, specifically ensuring the availability of key rotation. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that secrets and etcd encryption keys are rotated regularly to maintain security. While Kubernetes does not natively support automatic key rotation like cloud KMS services, it can be implemented through external secret management solutions or custom scripts. Additionally, integrating with external secret managers can facilitate key rotation.
